conversations.content: Today we studied the Campus Laboratory Safety and Hazardous Chemicals Control Regulations. If a student is found operating flammable or explosive reagents in violation of rules...
Playing with flammable reagents in the lab? That's suicidal! What's the first step when discovered?
then the lab technician immediately confiscates the reagents and stops the experiment, then removes the student from the current lab course.
Confiscate reagents, stop experiment, remove from course — strict enough! Any other dangerous situations?
If a fire alarm is triggered by the lab's fire detector, then cut the regional main power supply and activate the fire sprinkler system, then organize students to evacuate via the fire escape.
Fire alarm — cut power, activate sprinklers, then evacuate — life first! What's the third scenario?
If a leak is detected from a container of highly toxic chemicals, then turn on the emergency exhaust system, then cordon off the area and forbid personnel from approaching.
Toxic leak — exhaust plus cordon, don't get close! When any of these happens, what must ultimately be done?

If any of the above situations occurs, then the entire laboratory building must be locked down, the campus safety incident emergency mechanism must be activated, and the campus security office as well as the environmental protection department must be notified to intervene.

think: Campus lab safety & hazardous chemicals: three anomalies: student operation violation → confiscate reagents + stop experiment + remove from course; fire alarm → cut power + sprinklers + evacuate; toxic chemical leak → emergency exhaust + cordon. Any case → lockdown lab building + activate campus safety emergency + notify security & environmental protection.
memory: Campus Laboratory Safety and Hazardous Chemicals Control Regulations: If a student is found operating flammable or explosive reagents in violation of rules, then the lab technician immediately confiscates the reagents and stops the experiment, then removes the student from the current lab course; if a fire alarm is triggered by the lab's fire detector, then cut the regional main power supply and activate the fire sprinkler system, then organize students to evacuate via the fire escape; if a leak is detected from a container of highly toxic chemicals, then turn on the emergency exhaust system, then cordon off the area and forbid personnel from approaching; if any of the above situations occurs, then the entire laboratory building must be locked down, the campus safety incident emergency mechanism must be activated, and the campus security office as well as the environmental protection department must be notified to intervene.
